title: Concurrency test coverage: pool, exception isolation, ordering, no leaks, single-writer invariant
description: Add a focused test module that pins concurrency invariants we depend on across TASK-002..007. These tests guard against regressions where someone unwittingly introduces a parallel write path or a leak.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_concurrency.py exists and contains tests for: pool-size override, exception isolation per submitted task, monotonic processed counter across endpoints, no thread leaks after 100 SSE runs, no parallel writers to master.db.
All tests run in < 30 seconds total.
No flakiness across 10 consecutive runs (use pytest-randomly to randomize order).

steps.details: Move the AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE tests from TASK-001 into this file if not already there. Cover: default, override, clamp to 1, invalid string raises.

Submit 50 mock _compute_one callables to the pool; one raises ValueError. Drain results via as_completed. Assert: 49 successful results collected; 1 exception captured; pool remains usable for further submissions.

Drive /api/generate-apply-stream, /api/health, /api/classify, /api/auto-tag, /api/enrich-comments/stream with a mocked db. For each, capture the 'processed' field of every event; assert strict monotonic increase from 0 to total.

Snapshot threading.enumerate() before. Run /api/generate-apply-stream 100 times against a 10-track mocked db. Snapshot after. Assert that the set of thread names matches (modulo daemon threads). The pool persists (singleton) - threads inside it are expected; transient writer/cancel-poll threads must terminate.

Wrap pyrekordbox's db.session.commit() with a test-only monkeypatch that captures the calling thread and asserts only one distinct thread per SSE run ever calls commit. Run all 5 multi-track endpoints under this monkeypatch.

technicalNotes: pytest-randomly is already a dev-time tool; install if missing and add to [dev] extras. If not desired, run with `pytest -p no:randomly` for stable order, then a separate CI step shuffles.
The single-writer monkeypatch is the canonical guard - if any future PR adds a second writer thread, this test fires.
